Description: A Play-By-Mail Game, almost extinct, but play takes place by post (Averages are usually used or Refs may make dice rolls / card play for the players.
A Play-By-Email Game, a dying game type now, but was huge in the 90s. Turns are taken to describe the action, resolution is usually mediated by the Referee in their return.
Played online, turns take place within a Forum usually with at least a Thread devoted to the Game.
Played On-line in a Chatroom, Talker, or via a real-time messenger app.
How Roleplaying Games have been played since the 70s. Players describe actions with their voices, roll dice, and play cards on the table (other playing surfaces are available).
Playing characters via conferencing app. Card and Dice play may be verified by video, or handled by the Referee on a voice call.
Combining Forum / Chatroom and voice or video conferencing to allow both synchronous and asynchronous on-line play.
The game is played on-line, and in real-life. Maybe one or two characters Skype in from another location, while everyone else is at a table.
Play takes place through a dedicated gaming web app. Like this might become..
